title: Research Group
text:
Join Us
I am always looking for motivated PhD students and postdocs interested in molecular simulations, multiscale modelling, and interfacial physics.
Contact me at R.Pillai AT ed.ac.uk to discuss opportunities.

title: Welcome to my website
text:
Welcome to my personal website! This site is built with Quarto and hosted on GitHub Pages.
Here you’ll find information about my research, publications, and occasional blog posts about topics in computational fluid dynamics and multiscale modelling.
Stay tuned for more updates!

title: Rohit Pillai
text:
My group uses molecular simulations to study how freezing/boiling occurs, and how heat is transported at surfaces and interfaces, with applications from aircraft icing to designing next-generation cooling technologies.
  
    Explore research →
  
  
    
      Ice Nucleation & Growth
      How ice forms on surfaces, vibration effects on freezing, and the design of icephobic coatings
    
    
      Interfacial Thermofluids
      Thermal conductance at solid–liquid interfaces, surface wetting and self-cleaning, and nanomaterial-enhanced cooling
    
    
      Phase Change & Bubble Dynamics
      Vapour bubble nucleation, wettability effects on boiling, and acoustothermal nanobubble dynamics

title: Research
text: My research uses molecular simulation to understand interfacial phenomena that govern ice formation, heat flow, and phase change at the nanoscale. I lead a group at the University of Edinburgh funded by an ERC Starting Grant (NANO-COOL, 2024-2029). Our work falls into three themes, mapped on the phase diagram below.

Group Website

title: Teaching
text:
Third-year undergraduate course combining heat transfer and fluid mechanics. I redesigned this course in 2020-21, introducing:

MOOC-style lecture videos with integrated worked examples
Learning modules with curated real-world applications and research links
Design-focused laboratory exercises using real experimental data
Physical demonstrations (Stirling engines, radiometers, heat pipes)
Structured feedback system for consistent, actionable assessment




Second-year course covering mathematical methods for engineers, including differential equations and linear algebra.



First-year introductory engineering course providing foundation in core engineering principles.

title: Teaching
text:
Student Supervision

BEng/MEng Projects
I supervise 2-3 final-year BEng/MEng projects annually on topics including:

Molecular dynamics of phase change
Heat transfer at nanoscale interfaces
Surface acoustic wave effects on droplets and particles



Industrial Placements
Supervised 8 students on industrial placement programmes (2019-2021).

title: Teaching
text:
Personal Tutoring
Personal tutor to undergraduate students across all years of the MEng programme, providing academic guidance and pastoral support.
